videos.title: DeepMind’s New AI: A Gift To Humanity
videos.transcript_preview:
Google DeepMind gave an amazing gift to humanity. 
And it is full of surprises. Here’s why. Today,   we are living in the age of AI where these

Many of these solutions are proprietary,   require a subscription, a...
videos.ai_summary: Google DeepMind’s new Gemma 4 family offers surprisingly powerful, fully open‑source LLMs that can run locally on modest hardware—including phones, browsers, and even an original Nintendo Switch—enabling offline translation, summarization, image classification, and fine‑tuning without costly subscriptions. This release contrasts with many proprietary models that require cloud services and recurring fees, reinforcing the case for owning and deploying AI models independently. The Gemma 4 lineup also includes a 31‑billion‑parameter model that outperforms much larger, closed‑source competitors, highlighting the potential of dense, efficiently designed open

videos.title: I Built an App Using Only English… Thanks to Base44 🤯
videos.transcript_preview: Today's AI app review is this one of bass44.com. So bass44.com is just a AI natural language app that I came across this week and it caught my attention because of how flexible it is and just how many apps you can build on it. So I haven't signed up for it yet. I have not used it yet. I'm going to b...
videos.ai_summary: The review highlights bass44.com as an AI natural language app that allows users to build functional apps quickly without coding, making it accessible for non-technical users. The app offers basic features like message credits and integration credits, enabling users to create simple tools such as dashboards or CRMs. While effective for small-scale projects, scaling these apps often requires additional coding expertise, making bass44 a good starting point for quick, conversational app development.

videos.title: I Tried Zoom AI So You Don’t Have To
videos.transcript_preview: Okay, great. So, for today's quick AI tool review is going to be the Zoom AI companion. That's what Zoom calls it. I know most people don't really use Zoom much these days, right? So, I randomly found myself opening the app and I came across this AI companion. And one of the nice things that I liked...
videos.ai_summary: Zoom’s AI Companion is an integrated LLM‑based assistant that helps with meeting note‑taking, brainstorming, answering questions, and summarizing discussion points in real time.
